title: Memory-store comparison — workflowsPiExtension vs WASC
question: How do the two projects' Claude memory stores compare, objectively, by inventory and discipline?
method: full read of every memory file in both stores; substance mapping (common / only-A / only-B / near-match); index-integrity reconciliation.
findings_summary: A (workflowsPiExtension) = 107 files (103 feedback + 2 reference + 1 appendix + MEMORY.md); B (WASC) = 48 (41 feedback + 6 project + MEMORY.md). Substance mapping: 33 disciplines common to both, 65 only in A, 13 only in B, 8 near-match pairs. Index integrity: A's MEMORY.md has 106 bullet lines for its 106 non-MEMORY files (1:1); B's has 46 bullets for 47 non-MEMORY files — feedback-agent-time-not-human-time.md is on disk in B but absent from its index, so that memory is unindexed. Both stores encode the same class of correction-derived behavioral discipline (directive-parsing and scope, decision-discipline, verification rigor, persistence discipline, agent-dispatch posture, git hygiene); the memory infrastructure is identical, the difference is contents and index currency.
